items.title: How I orchestrated 3 LLMs to ship a plugin in 2 hours
items.why_it_matters: An indie developer shares how they used multiple LLMs to build and ship a working browser plugin in just two hours. It shows how fast you can go from idea to shipped product when you let AI do the heavy lifting while you focus purely on direction. Try setting a timer for two hours tonight and use Claude Code to build and ship one tiny, single-purpose feature for AI-ingest.

items.title: Ccgs: Share and resume Claude Code sessions via Git branches
items.why_it_matters: This tool lets you save, share, and resume your Claude Code terminal sessions by storing them directly in your project's Git repository. Since you are building AI-ingest and practicing Git discipline, this keeps your AI context history organized and version-controlled alongside your actual code commits. Try installing ccgs today to back up your current Claude Code session into a Git branch before your next big feature push.

items.title: PSA: Claude Code using Anthropic API keys will bill your API credits, not your subscription
items.why_it_matters: If you have an active Anthropic API key set in your environment variables, Claude Code and other local tools will default to charging your pay-as-you-go developer API account instead of utilizing your flat-rate Claude Pro/Max subscription. This can quietly drain your developer wallet while you are vibe coding. Try checking your local .env files and terminal environment variables today to ensure you aren't accidentally burning API credits on tasks your subscription should cover.
